female_female_dialogue_examples:
DENVER: All this time you were on the bridge?
BELOVED: No. After. When I got out.
DENVER: Why'd you come here?
BELOVED: To see her face.
DENVER: Ma'am's? Sethe's?
BELOVED: Yes. Sethe.
DENVER: You my sister, ain't you? You really are.

BELOVED: No. After. When I got out.
DENVER: Why'd you come here?
BELOVED: To see her face.
DENVER: Ma'am's? Sethe's?
BELOVED: Yes. Sethe.
DENVER: You my sister, ain't you? You really are.
DENVER: You won't leave us, will you?

DENVER: Why'd you come here?
BELOVED: To see her face.
DENVER: Ma'am's? Sethe's?
BELOVED: Yes. Sethe.
DENVER: You my sister, ain't you? You really are.
DENVER: You won't leave us, will you?
BELOVED: No. Never. This is where I am.

event_evidence: The mention of Sweet Home and the context of slavery.

evidence_snippets: Sethe is described as a Black woman with a 'sweet face.'
Denver is described as a Black girl.
Paul D. Garner is described as a Black man.
Sethe is a black woman who kills her own children to protect them.
C.U. OF A WAGON WHEEL MOVING RAPIDLY ON A ROAD. CAMERA PANS UP TO THE MAN DRIVING THE WAGON - A STERN WHITE MAN WEARING A DISTINCTIVE HAT...
